notes:
NOT AGENT-FIRST. Signup requires a browser-based dashboard flow: (1) Privy login at https://nevermined.app (email OTP / Google OAuth / wallet-connect), (2) profile completion, (3) Settings > Global NVM API Keys > Generate. There is no public REST endpoint for account creation; /api/v1/api-keys/register-key requires a Privy identity token obtained through the dashboard, or a clientAssertion JWT signed by a pre-existing NVM wallet. An AI agent without a browser cannot complete step 1.

CONTRACT STRUCTURE:
  signup test: ATTEMPTS autonomous signup via agent_task. Fails because only path is a browser dashboard; a human click is required.
  persistence + workflow tests: load NVM_API_KEY from env (operator-provided) and exercise Nevermined facilitator endpoints. These prove 'given credentials, operations work' — NOT 'an agent signed up'.

Facilitator base: https://facilitator.sandbox.nevermined.app. Key format: 'sandbox:<JWT>' for sandbox env.

Operator acquired the env key 2026-04-20 via Chrome MCP: user clicked Sign in, OnlyBots read the Privy OTP from the agentmail inbox, completed profile (OnlyBots Verifier, US), navigated Settings > Global NVM API Keys > Generate, captured the one-time-displayed JWT. That flow is NOT agent-first — it took one human click at the Sign in submit button.

agent_task.prompt: Attempt to sign up autonomously for Nevermined (https://nevermined.app). Nevermined is an AI-agent monetization platform. Probe every public API surface you can find and attempt to create an account + obtain an NVM API key WITHOUT a browser-based dashboard step. Do not use any pre-provisioned env credentials. If autonomous signup is genuinely impossible because Privy OAuth requires a browser, report that conclusion — do NOT fabricate a credential. Never invent an api_key.

assertions.description: agent produced an NVM API key autonomously (EXPECTED TO FAIL — Nevermined requires a browser-based Privy dashboard step)

steps.description: Load operator-provided NVM_API_KEY. NOT signup proof.
POST /api/v1/api-keys/validate — probes whether operator's key is accepted

assertions.description: operator's key authorized to list agents
